blocks.content: Arius approached Shirone and asked for a handshake, saying,

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: I must be going now. I can't be away from my post for long. Since Shirone is also an Unlocker, being aware of these matters will help you in your role as a unique in the magic society.
Shirone quickly grasped Arius's hand.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. It was incredibly helpful. Thank you.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Haha! There's no need to thank me. From now on, I plan to dig into your Ataraxia.
Arius flashed a cold smile, but Shirone couldn't see his expression. His head had already drooped deeply, succumbing to sleep.
High-level sleep magic cuts off consciousness so cleanly that it leaves no awareness of the mental waves being severed.
Even if Shirone's consciousness revived and tried to connect with the lost memory, he wouldn't feel anything unusual.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Well then, shall we begin?
Arius, a Scale Mage adept in handling the psyche realm, used a magic called Dive to penetrate the human consciousness.
He can infiltrate anywhere in the 12 levels of the human psyche, except for the 12th level shell which is the realm of intellect.
However, the inside of the human mind is a perilous environment. Diving recklessly could cost a person their life, even if they had ten lives to spare.
Thus, most divers use a method of throwing a net from the surface to extract only the desired information, and the keyword plays a crucial role in that.
Having suddenly implanted the keyword into Shirone, prompting him to recall the impression of Ataraxia, Arius did some stretches before casting the Dive magic.
Even now, dozens of divers lose their lives each year in the human psyche realm, but as a mage of Seven Prodigies, he cast the magic without hesitation.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Dive. Ataraxia.
The Deviate-Type Spirit Zone shrank to an extreme scale and penetrated Shirone's mind.
At the same moment, Arius' body vanished from the waiting room.
He reappeared after about five minutes, kneeling, his clothes drenched as though he had emerged from water.
Arius caught his breath and slowly stood up.
He gazed at Shirone with a troubled expression for a long moment before moving,
but then a voice called from outside the door.

blocks.speaker: Unknown
blocks.content: Sir Shirone, the trial will begin shortly. Please come and attend.
Clicking his tongue, Arius used Flicker, a signature move of Scale Mages, and disappeared from the waiting room.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh?
Shirone blinked and looked around.
Although he had been asleep for five full minutes, the clean cut of his consciousness made it feel like those five minutes had never existed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He really moves like a ghost. Well, Armin was like that too.
When there was no response from Shirone even after some time, the attendant opened the door and entered.

blocks.speaker: Attendant
blocks.content: Sir Shirone, you must come out now.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, sorry. I'll be right there.
Shirone followed the attendant into the courtroom.
The prominent nobles of Kazra sat in the jury seats, and two chairs were placed side by side below the judge's seat.
Instead of evidence, various tools were laid out in the evidence storage area, and an alchemist and a mage were inspecting them.
Shirone sat in the chair on the right and waited.
From outside the courtroom, a booming voice echoed, and the door swung open.

blocks.speaker: Unknown
blocks.content: His Majesty Orkamph is entering!
As the nobles stood to greet him, Orkamph strode in with a dignified gait.
He paid no attention to the other nobles and observed Shirone's condition.
Though Arius had already made contact, it was impossible to know whether he had successfully replicated Ataraxia.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Let's begin.
When Arius, seated next to Shirone, gave the signal, the judge initiated the examination.
The mage used a syringe to draw blood from both individuals.
The moment the needle pierced the king's body, the atmosphere in the courtroom turned chilly.
After Shirone's blood was also drawn, an alchemist mixed both samples into a substance called Oxstamine.
As he shook the beaker, the liquid changed from a deep purple to a transparent water-like substance.

blocks.speaker: Alchemist
blocks.content: The blood mixed with oxytamine will be stored in a vacuum state for 3 days. If a positive reaction occurs, it's a biological parent, and if a negative reaction occurs, it's not a biological parent.
As the alchemist handed over the blood, the judge put it into an alchemy box called a Caspi.
When the lid was closed, the air was expelled, creating a vacuum state.
It was perfect for security-required inspections because it would break if even a little air seeped in.

blocks.speaker: Judge
blocks.content: This Caspi will go into the vault and be placed in the inner palace garden. The royal guard will monitor it 24 hours a day, and any noble with access to the inner palace may check it at any time.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone nodded at the Inspection Department's decision.
If the vault was exposed to an unspecified number of people, they would become each other's observers, preventing any mischief.

blocks.speaker: Judge
blocks.content: This concludes the examination regarding the verification of the first prince of the Kazra Kingdom.
The procedure ended with the judge striking the gavel.
Orkamph left the courtroom as if he had urgent business to attend to, without even saying a word of appreciation to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He understood that, as a legal representative, she had to approve all administrative work, leaving her incredibly busy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was also the reason he could spend his time leisurely like a nobleman.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Still, wasn't that why he had brought Amy along?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was strange that there had been no message from her, no matter how far the lodging was.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: You failed?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: I apologize. I succeeded in casting the net, but I couldn't replicate it.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Replication is possible even now. It's just that there's no need to do so.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: No need?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Replication is possible even now. It's just that there's no need to do so. Ataraxia was encapsulated. Encapsulated? Is it a type of code? No. Code is about rules. You write 100 characters on a 1-meter strip of paper, wrap it around a rod of a specific thickness, and read the characters that align on one side. If a sentence is revealed, it is a code. Now, what is encapsulation? You write a clear sentence on the 1-meter strip of paper and then crumple it up randomly. There's no pattern, and it cannot be unraveled. What I saw wasn't Ataraxia, but rather a tangled cloud of information, like a ball of yarn.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Then what do you plan to do now? We absolutely need Ataraxia.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: First, I suggest speaking with Shirone. To find a solution, I need to see Ataraxia directly—learn what kind of information system it is, and understand its activation principle.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: A demonstration of Ataraxia...
Shirone was a clever child, so there was a high probability that he would guess that he wasn't simply invited to the royal palace for a paternity test.
In that situation, if the original purpose was brought up, the damage Shirone would receive would be tremendous.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Isn't it too blatant? He'll definitely be suspicious.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Your Majesty, this is a major matter that will change the first prince. The Terraje faction will surely make their move within the three days before the test results are out.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: If we do not take advantage of having priority in negotiations with Shirone, the initiative will be taken by the Terraje faction.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: If we're going to do this, we might as well make it grand, right?
Shirone, who was heading to his parents' quarters, stopped in his tracks on the 2nd floor corridor.
An unknown attendant, a messenger of Zion, was blocking the way.
It was simply amazing how he could find him like a ghost in this vast royal palace.

blocks.speaker: Attendant
blocks.content: Sir Shirone, you must come with me.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Please lead the way.
The attendant took Shirone to the basement.
It was a place where warehouses were concentrated, so there was very little foot traffic, and perhaps because expansion construction was frequent, the layout was complicated like a maze.
They arrived at the outermost part, where a door was installed into the wall, leading even deeper inside.
It was not a room that had originally existed.
This is the place. Please come in.
Shirone grabbed the doorknob without lowering his guard.
Contrary to his expectation of a room, what lay before him was a rectangular cave lit by torches, stretching out with another door visible 15 meters ahead.
A double entrance.
It didn't seem like a structure chosen for fun, but no reasonable explanation came to mind either.
When he was about halfway through the cave, the attendant slammed the door shut with a thud.
It was clearly to alert someone.
Startled, Shirone stopped in his tracks, but after taking a deep breath, he opened the second door.
It was a completely white room.
Not painted, but made entirely of marble, and along the walls, shelves were lined with various objects on display.
There didn't seem to be anything particularly luxurious, but knowing Zion's nature, the room still gave off a sense of unease.
Zion and Uorin were drinking tea at the table.
Seeing Uorin, he felt a little relieved, but realizing that even such thoughts were dangerous, he erased them from his mind.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: You're here. What are you doing standing there? Come in.
Shirone hesitated to move.
Zion and Uorin were looking at him with playful eyes.
But he couldn't just stand at the door, so he cautiously took a step forward, being mindful of his surroundings.
At that moment, the floor suddenly sank with a whoosh, and his body toppled forward.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ugh! What the—!
Shirone hurriedly raised his head.
The floor was soft, so he didn't feel any impact, but hearing Zion and Uorin's giggles made his face turn red with embarrassment.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why is the floor so soft?
Crawling and groping around, he felt a solid floor at some boundary.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Are you okay, Oppa? I'm sorry for teasing you. But it was fun, wasn't it?
Uorin approached and held out his hand.
Grabbing her hand and standing up, Shirone looked back at the spot where he had fallen.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What's this?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: It's <Fluffy Everywhere>.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Fluffy... what?
